title: Mesosphaerum coccineum
taxonomy: Mesosphaerum coccineum is a species within the Lamiaceae family, commonly known as the mint family. This classification indicates that Mesosphaerum coccineum shares characteristics with other plants in the Lamiaceae family, such as similar leaf morphology and inflorescence structure. The Lamiaceae family is further divided into various subfamilies and tribes, and Mesosphaerum coccineum is likely to be found within a specific subfamily or tribe within this family. The exact classification of Mesosphaerum coccineum may vary depending on the source or taxonomy used. Further research is needed to determine the most accurate and up-to-date classification of this species.
uses: Mesosphaerum coccineum has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in Brazilian folk medicine, due to its potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. Its leaves and stems are used to create infusions and decoctions that are applied topically or consumed as remedies. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Mesosphaerum coccineum for medicinal use. However, its traditional use suggests potential in treating various health conditions.

properties: Mesosphaerum coccineum, also known as red button sage, is a plant with various therapeutic properties. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat digestive issues and relieve stress. The plant's leaves and flowers contain essential oils that have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for wound healing and skin conditions. Some studies suggest that Mesosphaerum coccineum may have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can help protect against infections and oxidative stress. constituents: Mesosphaerum coccineum contains sesquiterpene lactones, including coccineum lactone, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Other active constituents of Mesosphaerum coccineum include flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant's sesquiterpene lactones have also shown potential as antimalarial agents.
